subject: Reading
description: Tests retrieval of facts and details stated explicitly in the passage.
common_trap: Students pick an option that sounds plausible instead of checking the exact wording of the text.

subject: Reading
description: Tests drawing a conclusion from clues that are implied but never directly stated.
common_trap: Students choose an answer that is true in general but not actually supported by the passage.

subject: Reading
description: Tests identifying the central point of a passage or paragraph rather than its details.
common_trap: Students select a frequently mentioned detail instead of the idea that covers the whole text.

subject: Reading
description: Tests recognising the underlying message or lesson the writer conveys through the story.
common_trap: Students restate the plot instead of the broader idea the events illustrate.

subject: Reading
description: Tests working out the meaning of a word or phrase as it is used in the passage.
common_trap: Students choose the most common dictionary meaning instead of the meaning that fits the context.

subject: Reading
description: Tests identifying the narrator's or writer's tone and attitude towards the subject.
common_trap: Students confuse a character's emotion with the tone of the narration itself.

subject: Reading
description: Tests explaining why the writer used a technique such as simile or repetition and what effect it creates.
common_trap: Students name the device correctly but choose a vague generic effect that does not fit this passage.

subject: Reading
description: Tests recognising how the passage is organised such as by contrast or by cause and effect.
common_trap: Students describe what the text says rather than how it is arranged.

subject: Reading
description: Tests identifying why the author wrote the text such as to persuade or to entertain.
common_trap: Students confuse the topic of the text with the author's reason for writing it.

subject: Reading
description: Tests working out why a character acts or speaks the way they do.
common_trap: Students choose what the character did rather than the reason behind the action.

subject: Reading
description: Tests tracking how a character's feelings or views shift across the passage.
common_trap: Students describe only the character's final state and miss the shift from the beginning.

subject: Reading
description: Tests identifying the mood created by the description of a place or time.
common_trap: Students choose a literal detail about the location instead of the feeling it creates.

subject: Reading
description: Tests recognising who is telling the story and how that shapes what the reader knows.
common_trap: Students confuse a first-person narrator with the author of the text.

subject: Reading
description: Tests placing events from the passage in the order they actually happened.
common_trap: Students use the order events are mentioned even when the text includes a flashback.

subject: Reading
description: Tests selecting the quotation or line that best supports a given statement.
common_trap: Students pick a quote on the same topic that does not actually prove the statement.

subject: Reading
description: Tests interpreting the pictures a poem creates through sensory language.
common_trap: Students read figurative lines literally and choose a surface-level meaning.

subject: Reading
description: Tests identifying the speaker's mood or attitude across a poem or stanza.
common_trap: Students judge tone from one striking word instead of the poem as a whole.

subject: Reading
description: Tests recognising when an object or image in a poem stands for a bigger idea.
common_trap: Students choose the literal object rather than the idea it represents.

subject: Reading
description: Tests comparing ideas or attitudes or techniques across two related extracts.
common_trap: Students answer correctly about one text but ignore what the other text says.

subject: Reading
description: Tests locating and interpreting information in factual texts such as articles and notices.
common_trap: Students stop at the first matching keyword instead of reading the full condition or exception.

subject: Reading
description: Tests choosing the word or phrase that fits the grammar of a gap in a passage.
common_trap: Students pick a word that fits the sentence alone but breaks tense or agreement with the surrounding text.

subject: Reading
description: Tests choosing the word that best fits the meaning and register of a gap in a passage.
common_trap: Students choose a near-synonym that clashes with the tone or collocations around the gap.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests accurate addition subtraction multiplication and division with whole numbers.
common_trap: Students misapply order of operations or make regrouping errors under time pressure.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests understanding the value of digits and comparing or rounding large numbers.
common_trap: Students confuse the digit itself with its value in that position.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests finding factors multiples and using highest common factor or lowest common multiple.
common_trap: Students mix up factors with multiples or forget that 1 and the number itself are factors.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests division problems where the remainder carries meaning such as leftover items or cycles.
common_trap: Students round the quotient instead of interpreting what the remainder means in context.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests comparing simplifying and operating with fractions of numbers and quantities.
common_trap: Students add numerators and denominators separately instead of finding a common denominator.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests ordering rounding and calculating with decimal numbers.
common_trap: Students judge size by the number of digits so they think 0.25 is greater than 0.5.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests finding percentages of quantities and converting between fractions decimals and percentages.
common_trap: Students apply the percentage to the wrong base amount especially after an increase or discount.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests sharing quantities in a ratio and scaling recipes maps and mixtures.
common_trap: Students divide by the number of ratio parts incorrectly or attach shares to the wrong person.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests reasoning with speed price per unit and other quantities compared per one unit.
common_trap: Students divide the two quantities the wrong way round and get the inverted rate.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests reading clocks and calculating durations and start or finish times.
common_trap: Students subtract times as if there were 100 minutes in an hour.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests reading transport or event timetables to find durations connections and best options.
common_trap: Students read the wrong row or column or ignore that a service does not run at the needed time.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests calculating totals change discounts and best-buy comparisons with money.
common_trap: Students compare prices without converting to the same quantity or unit first.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests converting between metric units of length mass and capacity.
common_trap: Students multiply when they should divide because they forget which unit is larger.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests measuring estimating and calculating with lengths and distances.
common_trap: Students mix units mid-calculation such as adding metres to centimetres directly.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests finding areas of rectangles triangles and composite shapes.
common_trap: Students confuse area with perimeter or forget to split a composite shape before calculating.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests finding the distance around straight-sided shapes including composite ones.
common_trap: Students leave out hidden or unlabelled sides of an L-shaped figure.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests finding volume and capacity of rectangular prisms and counting unit cubes.
common_trap: Students count only the visible cubes in a diagram and miss the hidden ones.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests estimating measuring and calculating angles on lines and inside shapes.
common_trap: Students assume a diagram is drawn to scale instead of using angle rules.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests identifying lines of symmetry and completing symmetrical figures.
common_trap: Students count rotational matches as extra lines of symmetry.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests recognising and applying translations reflections and rotations of shapes.
common_trap: Students reflect a shape across the wrong axis or confuse a rotation with a reflection.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests plotting and reading points on a grid and reasoning about positions.
common_trap: Students swap the across and up values and plot the reversed point.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests continuing number or shape patterns and finding a rule for later terms.
common_trap: Students find the step between terms but extend by counting one term short.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests reasoning about relationships between unknown quantities described in words or symbols.
common_trap: Students assign a number too early instead of comparing the quantities in general.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests finding the unknown number that makes a statement or diagram true.
common_trap: Students apply the operation instead of its inverse when working back to the unknown.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests solving simple equations and balancing both sides of a number sentence.
common_trap: Students change one side of the equation without doing the same to the other.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests reading comparing and combining values presented in tables.
common_trap: Students read the correct row but the wrong column or ignore the table's units.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests reading and comparing values shown in column or bar graphs.
common_trap: Students misread the scale when columns fall between labelled gridlines.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests interpreting trends and specific values on line graphs over time.
common_trap: Students read the value at the wrong point or confuse the steepness of a line with its height.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests interpreting proportions and calculating quantities from sector graphs.
common_trap: Students treat a sector's percentage as the actual quantity without using the total.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests calculating and reasoning with the mean of a data set including reverse problems.
common_trap: Students forget that changing one value changes the total and recompute the mean incorrectly.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests describing and comparing the likelihood of events as fractions or in words.
common_trap: Students count favourable outcomes but forget to count the total number of outcomes.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests counting arrangements combinations or cases in an organised way.
common_trap: Students list cases at random and either miss possibilities or count some twice.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests undoing a chain of operations from a final result to find the starting value.
common_trap: Students apply the original operations forward instead of inverting them in reverse order.

subject: Mathematical Reasoning
description: Tests combining several operations and pieces of information to solve a worded problem.
common_trap: Students stop after an intermediate step and choose that partial answer.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests locating the main claim an argument is trying to establish.
common_trap: Students choose a reason or piece of evidence instead of the claim it supports.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests finding the statement that is offered as support for the conclusion.
common_trap: Students choose background information rather than a statement doing supporting work.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests choosing the new statement that most supports the argument's conclusion.
common_trap: Students choose a statement that merely repeats the conclusion instead of adding support.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests choosing the new statement that most undermines the argument's conclusion.
common_trap: Students choose a statement that attacks the topic in general rather than the specific reasoning.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests finding the unstated idea the argument needs in order to work.
common_trap: Students choose a stated reason instead of the missing link that is never said.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests spotting the mistake in reasoning that makes an argument unsound.
common_trap: Students say the conclusion is false instead of explaining why the reasoning fails.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests judging whether given evidence actually supports a claim and how strongly.
common_trap: Students accept evidence that is related to the topic but does not bear on the claim.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests distinguishing genuine causes from things that merely happen together.
common_trap: Students assume that because two things occur together one must cause the other.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests selecting the conclusion best supported by a set of given facts.
common_trap: Students choose a conclusion that goes further than the facts allow.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests identifying what necessarily follows from a set of statements.
common_trap: Students choose what is probably true rather than what is guaranteed.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests identifying which option contradicts the given statements.
common_trap: Students reject an option that is merely unstated rather than actually impossible.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests reasoning correctly with if-then statements and their consequences.
common_trap: Students reverse the conditional and assume the converse must also hold.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests combining two or more general statements to reach a valid conclusion.
common_trap: Students let real-world knowledge override what the premises actually allow.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests placing people or objects in sequence from a set of positional clues.
common_trap: Students fix an order from the first clues and never test it against the later ones.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests deducing relative rank such as fastest or tallest from comparative clues.
common_trap: Students treat a relative clue as an exact position and over-commit early.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests reasoning about membership and overlap between groups described in words.
common_trap: Students assume all members of one group belong to another when only some do.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests reasoning about counts and regions where categories overlap.
common_trap: Students double-count items that belong to more than one category.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests deducing who is honest and who is lying from their statements.
common_trap: Students take one speaker's statement at face value instead of testing each possibility.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests spotting the numeric rule linking terms in an unfamiliar sequence or array.
common_trap: Students match the first two terms to a rule and never check it on the rest.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests working out the hidden operation a machine or code applies to inputs.
common_trap: Students find a rule that fits one example and assume it fits all of them.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests multi-step numeric reasoning where the method must be devised not just applied.
common_trap: Students grab the numbers and apply a familiar operation without modelling the situation.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests deducing missing information or conclusions from structured numeric tables.
common_trap: Students answer from a single cell instead of cross-checking rows and totals.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests judging whether the given information is enough to answer a question.
common_trap: Students solve for one possible answer and assume that means the data is sufficient.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests finding the rule in a sequence of shapes and choosing what comes next.
common_trap: Students track only one feature such as shape and miss a second changing feature such as shading.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests applying the relationship between one pair of figures to a new figure.
common_trap: Students choose a figure that resembles the example rather than one that follows the same rule.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests decoding how symbols map to properties of figures and applying the mapping.
common_trap: Students match a symbol to the wrong property because two figures share several features.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests recognising a figure after it has been turned or mirrored.
common_trap: Students accept a mirror image as a rotation of the original figure.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests predicting hole positions or shapes when folded paper is punched and unfolded.
common_trap: Students unfold only one fold and forget the holes duplicate across every fold line.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests matching solids to their views from the top front or side and to their nets.
common_trap: Students forget that blocks hidden behind others still appear in a different view.

subject: Thinking Skills
description: Tests general visualisation such as assembling pieces or tracking positions in space.
common_trap: Students rely on gut impression instead of checking each option against the figure.

subject: Writing
description: Tests crafting an engaging story with structure character and controlled language.
common_trap: Students retell events without a complication or resolution so the story has no shape.

subject: Writing
description: Tests building a vivid picture of a person place or moment using precise sensory detail.
common_trap: Students list adjectives instead of selecting a few strong details that create a mood.

subject: Writing
description: Tests writing a reflective first-person entry with authentic voice and feeling.
common_trap: Students recount the day's events without showing the writer's private thoughts and reactions.

subject: Writing
description: Tests retelling an experience in clear sequence with personal comment and detail.
common_trap: Students chain events with then-and-then linking instead of varying pace and sentence openers.

subject: Writing
description: Tests arguing a clear position with organised reasons and persuasive techniques.
common_trap: Students state opinions repeatedly instead of developing each reason with evidence or examples.

subject: Writing
description: Tests exploring more than one side of an issue in a balanced and thoughtful way.
common_trap: Students slip into one-sided persuasion instead of genuinely weighing both perspectives.

subject: Writing
description: Tests writing a spoken address with audience awareness and rhetorical devices.
common_trap: Students write an essay with no sense of a listening audience or spoken rhythm.

subject: Writing
description: Tests writing a letter with appropriate form register and purpose for its recipient.
common_trap: Students ignore the recipient and purpose so the tone and content do not fit the task.

subject: Writing
description: Tests explaining a topic clearly with organised facts and an objective tone.
common_trap: Students drift into personal opinion instead of presenting organised information.

subject: Writing
description: Tests reporting an event with headline lead paragraph and factual detail in report style.
common_trap: Students tell the event as a story instead of front-loading who what when and where.

subject: Writing
description: Tests giving clear practical guidance organised for a reader who needs help.
common_trap: Students write vague general tips instead of specific actionable steps for the situation.

subject: Writing
description: Tests responding to an unusual stimulus by choosing and sustaining a fitting form and voice.
common_trap: Students ignore the stimulus after the first line so the response could fit any prompt.
